title: mentha longifolia var. salicina
intro: Mentha longifolia var. salicina, also known as apple mint or horse mint, is a subspecies of the long-leaved mint plant, characterized by its distinctive apple-like fragrance and taste. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to relieve digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and dyspepsia, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Mentha longifolia var. salicina include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
